---
title: "Data Exploration"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# The persistent panel is built by notebooks/pre_process.R -- one row
# per (school, cohort year) for schools that report data in every year.
panel <- read_csv(here("data", "processed", "panel.csv"),
                  show_col_types = FALSE)
```

## Phase 1 -- coverage map

Not every variable is populated in every cohort year (e.g. `MD_EARN_WNE_P10`
only exists for cohorts whose 10-yr post-entry window has closed). Before
any analysis we need to know which (year, variable) cells have real data.

```{r}
# The ~15 "core panel" variables tied to the research question:
# expensive-school premium controlling for selectivity. Everything
# downstream in the EDA operates on this subset rather than all 74 cols.
CORE_VARS <- c(
  "MD_EARN_WNE_P6", "MD_EARN_WNE_P10",
  "NPT4",
  "SAT_AVG", "ADM_RATE",
  "C150", "CDR3", "RPY_3YR_RT",
  "GRAD_DEBT_MDN",
  "UGDS", "PCTPELL", "FIRST_GEN",
  "CONTROL", "PREDDEG", "HBCU"
)

# Percent non-null by (year, variable) over the persistent panel.
coverage <- panel |>
  select(year, all_of(CORE_VARS)) |>
  pivot_longer(-year, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") |>
  group_by(year, variable) |>
  summarise(pct_nonnull = mean(!is.na(value)) * 100, .groups = "drop")

# Order variables so the "always-populated" ones sit at the bottom and
# the sparse / heavily-lagged ones sit at the top for readability.
var_order <- coverage |>
  group_by(variable) |>
  summarise(avg_cov = mean(pct_nonnull)) |>
  arrange(avg_cov) |>
  pull(variable)

# Heatmap: cohort year on x, variable on y, cell shaded by % non-null.
ggplot(coverage, aes(x = year,
                     y = factor(variable, levels = var_order),
                     fill = pct_nonnull)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "grey95", high = "steelblue",
                      limits = c(0, 100)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(min(coverage$year), max(coverage$year), by = 2)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Core variable coverage across the persistent panel",
    subtitle = "% of schools with non-null value, by cohort year",
    x = "cohort year", y = NULL, fill = "% non-null"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
```

Overall coverage by year -- the top rows are the best candidates for
the cross-sectional slice in Phase 3.

```{r}
coverage |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarise(mean_pct_nonnull = mean(pct_nonnull), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(mean_pct_nonnull)) |>
  print(n = 10)
```
